extraction.one_line_summary: HIT Jan attacked 8km from summit on Valle Santa Cristina, caught Kemna's breakaway, held off Arensman for maiden Grand Tour stage win
tactical_patterns: Astana tested CARAPAS early on Mortirolo with NIBALI probe despite no support, forcing pink jersey group to respond.
Bahrain Victorious worked to set up final climb despite no protected GC rider, suggesting stage hunting strategy.
BILBAO-LANDER teammate crash with 8km remaining suggests Bahrain communication breakdown under pressure on decisive climb.
ALMEIDA deliberately managed effort behind initial attacks, limiting losses to 15sec while NIBALI bled minute chasing.

rider_signals.HIT Jan: positive: won from late 8km attack after earlier Mortirolo presence, showing recovered form after previous Chicchone second place
rider_signals.ARENSMAN Thymen: positive: only 22 years old, bridged solo to stage contention from pink jersey group, finished 7sec behind winner
rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: positive: picked up 4 bonus seconds in photo finish while limiting losses, closed GC gap from 7sec to 3sec
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: isolated early attack on Mortirolo went nowhere, then lost nearly minute on final climb showing age
rider_signals.ALMEIDA Joao: neutral: controlled ride limiting to 15sec loss suggests playing longer GC game rather than defending 30sec gap
rider_signals.SIVAKOV Pavel: negative: braking problems causing crash reveals Ineos mechanical preparation issues despite controlling earlier
rider_signals.KEMNA Leonard: positive: Etna winner attacked solo between climbs, held 51sec advantage entering final climb before caught 8km out
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: neutral: present in early moves on Mortirolo but unable to sustain effort on Valle Santa Cristina
